author: niv20
description: Callout Studio is the only callout plugin you'll ever need to download. It offers regular callouts, inline callouts, and callout headings. Plus, you can easily design your own custom styles! - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Niv Libovitch
manifest.description: Manage callouts with custom types, Material icons, native context menu actions, and full light and dark mode color control.
manifest.name: Callout Studio

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
To eliminate the startup flash of unstyled callouts, the plugin now keeps a self-healing local CSS cache on your device. It safely stores only generated styling - never your vault content - and never leaves your machine.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/2.1.6...2.1.7

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
Fixed a synchronization bug where collapsing a callout via Obsidian's native arrow left the callout heading arrow added by the plugin out of sync.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/2.1.5...2.1.6

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
For callouts that aren't used anywhere in the vault, I've hidden the "Replace in vault" option from their three-dot menu, as it's redundant.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/2.1.4...2.1.5

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
To help maintain clean structure and consistency, going forward, the first ID must match the callout name. To clearly indicate this, the first ID is now marked with a lock icon and cannot be removed (no 'X' button next to it).

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
## Callout Studio 2.1.0
An update focused primarily on the Palette Editor, featuring a complete redesign, background gradients, and finer color control.

**Redesigned Palette Editor**
The editor modal now uses a two-column layout with a sticky Live Preview for instant visual feedback without scrolling. The separate "Advanced" mode was removed, integrating gradient controls directly into the main interface.

**Dual-Tone Background Gradients**
You can now apply two-color linear or radial background gradients with 8 directional presets, configured separately for light and dark modes, and fully supported in PDF export.

**Background Intensity Control**
A new Intensity slider lets you fine-tune the strength of the auto-generated background tint derived from your accent color.

> **Note on Removed Colors**
> We removed some built-in color presets that were too similar to Obsidian's default colors. Don't worry - your existing notes will look exactly the same after you update. The plugin saves the exact color codes directly in your notes, so nothing will break.
> If you edit an old callout that used a removed color, the setting will simply say "Deleted color". This is completely harmless. You can either leave it as it is, or pick a new color whenever you like.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/2.0.0...2.1.0

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
# 🎉 **Callout Studio 2.0.0**

A major update that greatly expands what you can do with callouts. moving from a single block style to **three** different ways to use them, along with global style controls and your own custom color palettes.

___

### Main Feature: Three ways to use the same callout

Now every callout can be displayed in **three different styles**: 

- **Block** - The classic callout you already know.
    
- **Heading** - Turn normal headings into styled callouts with an icon and color, including clean formatting and smart icon placement.
    
- **Inline** - Small "pills" inside a line of text.
    
___


### Custom color palette management

- Save and manage your own custom color palettes.
    
- Importing a JSON file merges new palettes without overwriting your existing ones.
    
___


### Custom right-click menu

- Drag and drop menu items to arrange them in whatever order works best for you.
    
- The context menu is always active, and you can control which items appear in it.
    
___


### More improvements

- **Welcome screen**. A new welcome screen for new users that explains the plugin's features.

- **Global style for all callouts**. A new global settings screen that lets you control the look of all your callouts at once, including borders, rounded corners, size scale and more...

- **True Live Preview in the editor**. Real-time callout editing that looks exactly like it does in the final note.
    
- **Improved autocomplete**. Smart text matching in suggestions with a visual fade effect.
    
- **Export and Import**. Save everything — not just your callouts, but also your custom colors, preferences, and all other settings in a standard export file (`callout-studio-export.json`).
    
- **PDF Export**. Your callout colors now stay accurate and consistent when exporting notes to PDF.
    
- **Cleaner Interface**. Removed unnecessary icon source settings for a simpler and cleaner look.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.6.0...2.0.0

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
## What's New?

* **New Command:** Added the `Insert empty callout` command.
* **Improved `Wrap in callout`:** This command is now more forgiving. If triggered with no text selected, it will simply insert a new callout (instead of throwing an error). #8 



**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.5.0...1.6.0

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
**Smart callout fold controls in the context menu**

The right-click context menu now lets you set a callout's fold state directly: open (+), closed (-), or none. The menu detects the callout's current fold state and only shows the options that differ from it, so you never see a redundant action for the state you're already in.

Localization for the new fold actions was added across all 32 supported languages.


## What's Changed
* feat(context-menu): add callout fold state actions by @epilo9er in https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/pull/6

## New Contributors
* @epilo9er made their first contribution in https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/pull/6

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.4.0...1.5.0

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
**Align content with title:** new toggle under Settings → Global callout style that indents the callout body so it lines up with the title text rather than the icon. Works in Reading view, Live Preview, and PDF export, and is RTL-aware.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.3.1...1.4.0

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
## Icon Picker improvements
- The icon picker now opens with the currently selected icon pre-highlighted and scrolls it into view automatically
- Changing icon style (Outlined / Filled / Rounded / Sharp) or weight now instantly updates the preview without needing to reselect the icon

## Reliability fix
- Improved how Material Symbols font is loaded — injecting a Google Fonts stylesheet link for better reliability and performance
- Clarified the error message shown when an icon fails to download, making it clearer that the icon may be unavailable or that you're offline


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.3.0...1.3.1

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
Add emoji icon picker and PDF-safe icon rendering

Bundled emoji dataset with search and skin tone selection
New CSSInjector.paintIcons() bakes icons into DOM so they survive
Obsidian's PDF export (which drops adopted stylesheets)


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.2.2...1.3.0

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
- Remove "Show icon previews" / "Show color previews" toggles from Autocomplete settings. Both features are now **always on**.
- Remove the three per-item toggles from Context menu settings ("Edit" / "Open settings" / "Copy Markdown"). All items are now **always shown**.
- Each section retains only its master Enable/Disable toggle
- Cleaned up orphaned i18n keys from all 32 locale files

> [!note]
> Old `data.json` entries are handled automatically. Removed keys are ignored and cleaned up on save.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.2.1...1.2.2

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
Obsidian 1.13 introduces a breaking change to `--callout-color`: instead of raw RGB triplets (e.g. 255, 0, 0), it now requires a valid CSS color (e.g. #ff0000, rgb(...), red). This caused custom callouts generated by Callout Studio to lose their colors.

**Callout Studio now detects the Obsidian version at runtime and outputs the correct format automatically:**
- 1.13+ → full CSS color  
- ≤1.12 → RGB triplet (unchanged)

Obsidian 1.13 is currently still in Catalyst (internal testing), but this ensures full compatibility once it is officially released.

Fixes #5

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
feat: allow spaces in callout IDs

Callout IDs can now be human-readable, multi-word labels with spaces (e.g. `> [!Multi Word Callout]`)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.1.4...1.2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.1.0...1.1.1

## 🐛 Bug fixes

- **Fixed SVG icon rendering on iPadOS**: icons now display correctly using `flex-shrink` and `transform` adjustments.
- **Improved color input swatches**: color swatches now render consistently across devices.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.0.3...1.1.0

## Callout Studio 1.1.0 — Multi-language support 🌍

### What's new
- **30 new UI languages** — Arabic, Bulgarian, Chinese (Simplified & Traditional),
  Czech, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hindi, Hungarian,
  Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Malay, Norwegian Bokmål, Persian,
  Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Russian, Spanish, Swedish, Thai, Turkish,
  Ukrainian, and Vietnamese (joining English and Hebrew).
- **Manual language picker** — a new **Language** section in settings lets you
  choose a display language directly, listed under each language's own native
  name. The default, **Automatic**, keeps following Obsidian's interface language.
- **Smarter locale matching** — regional variants like `zh-tw` / `zh-hk` now
  resolve correctly before falling back to the base language.

### Notes
- All translations except English were generated with AI assistance and may
  contain errors. Corrections via pull request or email are very welcome — see
  the Localization section of the README.
- Missing translations gracefully fall back to English.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.0.2...1.0.3

The Material icon picker is now fully responsive for mobile devices and narrow screens. On devices with limited screen width (600px or smaller), the search field expands to full width, while the style, weight, and category filters neatly wrap to a second row. On larger screens, all controls remain in a single row for efficient browsing. This improvement makes it significantly easier to discover and select Material Symbols icons on phones and tablets.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.0.1...1.0.2

Fixed two responsive design issues on narrow screens:

Editor modal stickiness — The Live Preview column was staying fixed at the top and overlapping the settings when the modal switched to a single-column layout. Now it scrolls naturally, keeping settings accessible.

Color grid spacing — The six color inputs (3 rows of light/dark pairs) were overlapping each other on mobile because iOS renders native color pickers as larger circular swatches. Added vertical padding, increased input size to 36×36px, and added row dividers so each color row has breathing room and is clearly visible.

Both issues only appear on viewports narrower than 700px; desktop layout is unchanged.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Changed

This release focuses on Obsidian plugin compliance fixes flagged in the community review of 1.0.0. No new features — all changes improve compatibility, supply-chain trust, and transparency.

### 🪟 Popout window compatibility
- Replaced all `document` references with `activeDocument` so popout/secondary windows render callouts correctly (26 sites across editor, settings, manager, and UI modules)
- Replaced `setTimeout` / `clearTimeout` with `activeWindow.setTimeout` / `activeWindow.clearTimeout` so timers attach to the correct window (8 sites)

### 🧩 Obsidian DOM helpers
- Migrated `document.createElement`, `document.createDocumentFragment`, `createEl("div")`, and `createEl("span")` calls to native Obsidian helpers (`createEl`, `createDiv`, `createSpan`, `createFragment`)

### 🎨 Styling
- Refined CSS selectors for better specificity and removed unnecessary `!important` declarations

### 🔐 Supply-chain & transparency
- Release artifacts (`main.js`, `styles.css`) now ship with [GitHub build provenance attestations](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-for-github-actions/using-artifact-attestations)
- Added a Permissions section to the README documenting API surface, clipboard access, and the single external network dependency (`fonts.gstatic.com` for Material Symbols icons)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Niv20/obsidian-Plugin-Callout-Studio/compare/1.0.0...1.0.1

releases.author: Niv20
releases.description:
Welcome to the initial release of **Callout Studio**! 🎉

Callout Studio is the ultimate callout manager for Obsidian. It lets you create, edit, and style your own callout types, override the built-in ones, pick icons from large libraries, and keep callouts under control across your whole vault.
releases.name: 1.0.0 - Initial Release
